text: Where is it?  Where is the city?  You have to find it.  You have to get back there.
Your steps resonate strangely.  It's as if you had done this a thousand times before.
You see yourself as a child, leaving through this very gate.  Why would you do that?  Why would you leave?
The empty streets stretch out before you, but you can't remember where they lead.  You can't remember where you were going.
You see yourself old and feeble.  Your servants carry you through the gate, but you don't want to go.  You want to stay here.
You kneel on the ground, and the world around you warps and shifts.  You hoped it would take you home, but it doesn't.
You hold the medallion in your hand, and do the sign for home.  Why doesn't it work?
You see yourself standing before a great gate, but you can't remember what's on the other side.
You step through and into the highroad, expecting home on the other side.  But it's not there.
You step forward and the world around you shifts, but is it the one you know?  You can't remember.
You walk forward, so engrossed in memory you don't notice the landscape is gone.
The old statues mock you.  They tell you to hurry, but you don't know where to go.
The old conquerors stare with their empty eyes.  They know you don't belong here.
Some of the statues are broken, but you can't remember how they got that way.  You can't remember what happened here.
You feel like a thousand years have passed, but you can't remember a single moment of them.
How long have you walked?  Days?  Weeks?  Will they ever end?
The highroad is crumbling.  How did it ever get like this?
It should have ended now.  It was never this long.
Maybe death will find you soon.  Like it found everyone else.
No one deserves this.  Why do you?
Please end.  Anywhere but here.  Please end.  Please end.
You mutter to yourself, but your voice is distorted and strange.  You can't understand what you're saying.
The fields turn red with blood, but you can't remember whose it is.  You can't remember what happened.
Something terrible has happened.  It's no surprise you can't remember.
Why would fate punish you like this?  Why would you survive, only to walk endlessly through the highroad ruins?
You deserve this.  You caused this.
Why are you alone here?  You had never seen it like this before-
How could the highroad break?  Where is everyone?
You cry fitfully, but the highroad doesn't care.  It just keeps going.
You fear you'll never see the pale blue sun again.  No, you know you won't.
You think to voice a prayer, but the words are lost on your tongue.
These steps led home.  Why don't they anymore?  What has changed?
It feels like pushing through cobwebs, but the highroad pulls you forward.
You once knew the name of these lands.  Why can't you remember it?
Did the gods abandon us?  Did they ever care about us at all?
You remember banners fluttering before this very gate.  What did they look like?  What did they say?
You remember the bustling streets and grand buildings, did you ever call one home?
